comment_text.sn20.8:2.2: For Ajātasau’s avowed wish to invade the Vajjis see DN 16:1.1.1.

root_text.sn20.8:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā vesāliyaṁ viharati mahāvane kūṭāgārasālāyaṁ.
root_text.sn20.8:2.1: “Kaliṅgarūpadhānā, bhikkhave, etarahi licchavī viharanti appamattā ātāpino upāsanasmiṁ.
root_text.sn20.8:2.2: Tesaṁ rājā māgadho ajātasattu vedehiputto na labhati otāraṁ na labhati ārammaṇaṁ.
root_text.sn20.8:2.3: Bhavissanti, bhikkhave, anāgatamaddhānaṁ licchavī sukhumālā mudutalunahatthapādā
root_text.sn20.8:2.4: te mudukāsu seyyāsu tūlabibbohanāsu yāvasūriyuggamanā seyyaṁ kappissanti.
root_text.sn20.8:2.5: Tesaṁ rājā māgadho ajātasattu vedehiputto lacchati otāraṁ lacchati ārammaṇaṁ.
root_text.sn20.8:3.1: Kaliṅgarūpadhānā, bhikkhave, etarahi bhikkhū viharanti appamattā ātāpino padhānasmiṁ.
root_text.sn20.8:3.2: Tesaṁ māro pāpimā na labhati otāraṁ na labhati ārammaṇaṁ.
root_text.sn20.8:3.3: Bhavissanti, bhikkhave, anāgatamaddhānaṁ bhikkhū sukhumā mudutalunahatthapādā.
root_text.sn20.8:3.4: Te mudukāsu seyyāsu tūlabibbohanāsu yāvasūriyuggamanā seyyaṁ kappissanti.
root_text.sn20.8:3.5: Tesaṁ māro pāpimā lacchati otāraṁ lacchati ārammaṇaṁ.

translation_text.sn20.8:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Vesālī, at the Great Wood, in the hall with the peaked roof.
translation_text.sn20.8:1.3: There the Buddha addressed the mendicants,
translation_text.sn20.8:2.1: “Mendicants, these days the Licchavis live using wood blocks as pillows, and they exercise diligently and keenly.
translation_text.sn20.8:2.2: King Ajātasattu of Magadha, son of the princess of Videha, finds no vulnerability, he’s got no opportunity.
translation_text.sn20.8:2.3: But in the future the Licchavis will become delicate, with soft and tender hands and feet.
translation_text.sn20.8:2.4: They’ll sleep on soft beds with down pillows until the sun comes up.
translation_text.sn20.8:2.5: King Ajātasattu of Magadha, son of the princess of Videha, will find a vulnerability and get an opportunity.
translation_text.sn20.8:3.1: These days the mendicants live using wood blocks as pillows, and they meditate diligently and keenly.
translation_text.sn20.8:3.2: Māra the Wicked finds no vulnerability, he’s got no opportunity.
translation_text.sn20.8:3.3: But in the future the mendicants will become delicate, with soft and tender hands and feet.
translation_text.sn20.8:3.4: They’ll sleep on soft beds with down pillows until the sun comes up.
translation_text.sn20.8:3.5: Māra the Wicked will find a vulnerability and get an opportunity.
translation_text.sn20.8:3.6: So you should train like this:
translation_text.sn20.8:3.7: ‘We will live using wood blocks as pillows, and we will meditate diligently and keenly.’

variant_text.sn20.8:2.3: sukhumālā → sukhumā (mr) | mudutalunahatthapādā → mudutalāhatthapādā (sya-all, km); mudutaluṇahatthapādā (pts1ed, pts2ed)
variant_text.sn20.8:2.4: tūlabibbohanāsu → tūlabimbohanādīsu (bj); thūlabimbohanāsu (sya-all, km); tulabimbohanāsu (pts1ed, pts2ed); tūlabibbohanādīsu (mr)
